story.iWant: every provider call instrumented to emit usage diagnostics to a shared async queue that drains to the cost-monitor
story.soThat: I can track per-provider and per-model costs, tokens, latency, errors, and success rates through the existing ICostTracker.generateReport() pipeline

task.title: Create DiagnosticsEvent type and DiagnosticsQueue in shared telemetry package

tasks.task: - [ ] Create packages/shared/src/telemetry/diagnostics-event.ts with DiagnosticsEventBase, ToolDiagnosticsEvent, ProviderDiagnosticsEvent discriminated union, DiagnosticsEventType, DiagnosticsErrorCode - [ ] Create packages/shared/src/telemetry/sanitize-error.ts with sanitizeErrorMessage() utility (truncate 500 chars, strip API key patterns) - [ ] Create packages/shared/src/telemetry/validate-diagnostics.ts with validateCostUsd(), validateTokenCount(), validateErrorCode() - [ ] Create packages/shared/src/telemetry/diagnostics-queue.ts with DiagnosticsEventProcessor and DiagnosticsQueue class (drainPromise guard, setProcessor guard, droppedCount) - [ ] Create packages/shared/src/telemetry/index.ts barrel export - [ ] Add telemetry subpath export to packages/shared/package.json - [ ] Update packages/shared/src/index.ts to re-export telemetry barrel - [ ] Write unit tests for DiagnosticsQueue (emit, drain, dispose, overflow, unref timer, drainPromise guard, setProcessor warning, droppedCount, structured error logging) - [ ] Write unit tests for sanitizeErrorMessage() - [ ] Write unit tests for value validation utilities

- [ ] Create packages/providers/src/instrumented-agent-provider.ts implementing IAgentProvider - [ ] Emit provider:call on entry, provider:complete on success, provider:error on failure - [ ] Capture latencyMs, success, costUsd, errorCode (typed as DiagnosticsErrorCode), errorMessage (sanitized), agentType, projectId, engineId, taskId, taskType - [ ] Use sanitizeErrorMessage() for all errorMessage assignments - [ ] Add updateContext() method for mutable taskId/taskType between calls - [ ] Delegate isAvailable() and dispose() to inner provider - [ ] Access result.tokens for token tracking (note: requires AgentTaskResult token field) - [ ] Write unit tests with mock inner provider and mock DiagnosticsQueue

task.title: Create InstrumentedLLMProvider decorator implementing full IAIProvider

tasks.task: - [ ] Create packages/providers/src/instrumented-llm-provider.ts implementing full IAIProvider interface - [ ] Implement initialize(), sendMessage() (streaming), sendMessageSync(), getCapabilities(), getModels(), dispose() - [ ] Instrument sendMessageSync() with provider:call/complete/error events - [ ] Instrument sendMessage() streaming: wrap AsyncIterable to track tokens incrementally and emit events on completion - [ ] Extract tokens from response.usage (inputTokens, outputTokens) - [ ] Add projectId and engineId to constructor context (matching InstrumentedAgentProvider) - [ ] Use sanitizeErrorMessage() for all errorMessage assignments - [ ] Do NOT expose isAvailable() -- IAIProvider does not define it - [ ] Add security boundary comments noting prompt content access - [ ] Write unit tests verifying full IAIProvider implementation, streaming instrumentation, no isAvailable() method

task.title: Update cost-monitor types and create provider name mapping

tasks.task: - [ ] Add 'opencode' | 'z-ai' | 'zen-mcp' to Provider type union in packages/cost-monitor/src/types.ts - [ ] Replace local AgentType definition with import from @tamma/shared - [ ] Re-export AgentType for backward compatibility - [ ] Create packages/providers/src/provider-name-mapping.ts with mapProviderName() safe validation function - [ ] Verify all existing references to Provider and AgentType still compile

task.title: Create diagnostics processor for cost tracking

tasks.task: - [ ] Create packages/providers/src/diagnostics-processor.ts - [ ] Map DiagnosticsEvent fields to UsageRecordInput fields - [ ] Use mapProviderName() instead of unsafe `as Provider` cast - [ ] Use sanitizeErrorMessage() for error messages - [ ] Apply value validation (costUsd >= 0, tokens in range, errorCode max 100 chars) - [ ] Call costTracker.recordUsage() for each provider:complete and provider:error event - [ ] Use structured error logging instead of silent swallow: logger?.warn() for processor errors - [ ] Write unit tests verifying correct UsageRecordInput mapping, mapProviderName usage, error resilience, value validation

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: DiagnosticsQueue.emit() is synchronous -- executeTask/sendMessageSync returns before the queue drains

InstrumentedAgentProvider emits provider:call, provider:complete, and provider:error DiagnosticsEvents with correct fields (providerName, model, agentType typed as AgentType, projectId, engineId, taskId, taskType, latencyMs, success, costUsd, errorCode typed as DiagnosticsErrorCode)
InstrumentedLLMProvider implements full IAIProvider interface: initialize(), sendMessage() (streaming), sendMessageSync(), getCapabilities(), getModels(), dispose()
InstrumentedLLMProvider sendMessage() wraps AsyncIterable to track tokens incrementally and emit provider:complete on stream end, provider:error on stream failure
InstrumentedLLMProvider does NOT expose isAvailable() (IAIProvider does not define it)
DiagnosticsProcessor maps DiagnosticsEvent to UsageRecordInput using mapProviderName() (not unsafe cast) and calls costTracker.recordUsage()
Processor failure uses structured error logging (logger?.warn) instead of silent swallow, and does NOT affect queued events or provider call flow
Provider type in cost-monitor includes 'opencode', 'z-ai', 'zen-mcp'
AgentType in cost-monitor is imported from @tamma/shared, not redefined locally
Queue overflow drops oldest event (shift) when maxQueueSize is reached and increments droppedCount
Queue drain timer is unref'd so it does not prevent process exit
DiagnosticsEvent is defined in diagnostics-event.ts (not inline in diagnostics-queue.ts) with discriminated union: ToolDiagnosticsEvent and ProviderDiagnosticsEvent
Context fields (agentType, projectId, engineId, taskId, taskType) are required on DiagnosticsEvent, not optional
setProcessor() logs warning via optional logger if processor already set (does not silently replace)
drainPromise guard prevents concurrent drain from timer + dispose() racing
errorMessage is sanitized via sanitizeErrorMessage() (truncate 500 chars, strip API key patterns)
errorCode is typed as DiagnosticsErrorCode union, not plain string
InstrumentedAgentProvider supports per-call taskId/taskType via updateContext() method
InstrumentedLLMProvider context includes projectId and engineId (matching InstrumentedAgentProvider)
Value validation: costUsd >= 0, token counts in [0, 10_000_000], errorCode truncated to 100 chars
getDroppedCount() exposes number of events dropped due to overflow
InstrumentedLLMProvider has security boundary comments noting it must NOT log or store prompt text

doc.title: Story 9-2: Provider Diagnostics Collector
doc.snippet: DiagnosticsEvent is defined in its own file diagnostics-event.ts with discriminated union pattern. Context fields agentType, projectId, engineId, taskId, taskType are required.

doc.title: Story 9-11: Diagnostics Queue canonical design
doc.snippet: DiagnosticsQueue with drainPromise guard, DiagnosticsEvent in separate file, required context fields.

doc.title: Technical Architecture
doc.snippet: All system actions are captured as immutable events; every operation must emit events for audit trail.

ref.title: IAgentProvider interface
ref.snippet: IAgentProvider { executeTask(config, onProgress?): Promise<AgentTaskResult>; isAvailable(): Promise<boolean>; dispose(): Promise<void>; }

ref.title: IAIProvider interface
ref.snippet: IAIProvider { initialize(config): Promise<void>; sendMessage(request, options?): Promise<AsyncIterable<MessageChunk>>; sendMessageSync(request): Promise<MessageResponse>; getCapabilities(): ProviderCapabilities; getModels(): Promise<ModelInfo[]>; dispose(): Promise<void>; }

ref.title: UsageRecordInput and ICostTracker
ref.snippet: UsageRecordInput = Omit<UsageRecord, 'id' | 'timestamp' | 'inputCostUsd' | 'outputCostUsd' | 'totalCostUsd'>; ICostTracker { recordUsage(usage: UsageRecordInput): Promise<UsageRecord>; generateReport(options): Promise<CostReport>; }

ref.title: AgentTaskResult
ref.snippet: AgentTaskResult { success: boolean; output: string; costUsd: number; durationMs: number; error?: string; tokens?: { input: number; output: number }; }

ref.title: Shared package barrel
ref.snippet: export * from './types/index.js'; export * from './utils/index.js'; export * from './contracts/index.js'; export * from './errors.js';

dep.reason: DiagnosticsQueue, DiagnosticsEvent, sanitizeErrorMessage, validateDiagnostics live here; AgentType imported from here
UsageRecordInput and ICostTracker for diagnostics processor; Provider type for mapProviderName
IAgentProvider and IAIProvider interfaces wrapped by decorators; mapProviderName utility

constraint.description: DiagnosticsQueue.emit() must be synchronous with zero async overhead in the hot path
DiagnosticsEvent is defined in diagnostics-event.ts as a discriminated union (ToolDiagnosticsEvent | ProviderDiagnosticsEvent), not inline in diagnostics-queue.ts
DiagnosticsQueue lives in packages/shared/src/telemetry/, NOT in mcp-client ToolHookRegistry
InstrumentedLLMProvider must implement full IAIProvider interface: initialize(), sendMessage(), sendMessageSync(), getCapabilities(), getModels(), dispose()
InstrumentedLLMProvider must NOT expose isAvailable() because IAIProvider does not define it
Processor failures must use structured error logging (logger?.warn) instead of silent swallow, and must not affect the queue or provider call flow
agentType field must be typed as AgentType from @tamma/shared, not string
errorCode must be typed as DiagnosticsErrorCode union, not plain string
Context fields (agentType, projectId, engineId, taskId, taskType) are required on DiagnosticsEvent, not optional
cost-monitor AgentType must be re-exported after importing from @tamma/shared for backward compatibility
Queue drain timer must be unref'd so Node.js process can exit cleanly
drainPromise guard prevents concurrent drain from timer + dispose() racing
sanitizeErrorMessage() must truncate to 500 chars and strip API key patterns (Bearer, sk-*, key-*) from all errorMessage fields
InstrumentedLLMProvider has access to prompt content via MessageRequest and must NOT log or store prompt text
mapProviderName() validates provider names against known set instead of unsafe 'as Provider' cast
setProcessor() must log warning via optional logger if processor already set, not silently replace
costUsd >= 0, token counts in [0, 10_000_000], errorCode truncated to 100 chars

interface.name: DiagnosticsEventBase
interface.signature: { type: DiagnosticsEventType; timestamp: number; agentType: AgentType; projectId: string; engineId: string; taskId: string; taskType: string; latencyMs?: number; success?: boolean; costUsd?: number; errorCode?: DiagnosticsErrorCode; errorMessage?: string; tokens?: { input: number; output: number }; }

interface.name: ToolDiagnosticsEvent
interface.signature: extends DiagnosticsEventBase { type: 'tool:invoke' | 'tool:complete' | 'tool:error'; toolName: string; serverName?: string; args?: Record<string, unknown>; }

interface.name: ProviderDiagnosticsEvent
interface.signature: extends DiagnosticsEventBase { type: 'provider:call' | 'provider:complete' | 'provider:error'; providerName: string; model?: string; }

interface.name: sanitizeErrorMessage
interface.signature: (message: string): string — truncates to 500 chars, strips API key patterns (Bearer, sk-*, key-*)

interface.name: DiagnosticsQueueLogger
interface.signature: { warn(message: string, context?: Record<string, unknown>): void; }

interface.name: DiagnosticsQueue
interface.signature: { constructor(options?: { drainIntervalMs?: number; maxQueueSize?: number }, logger?: DiagnosticsQueueLogger); setProcessor(processor: DiagnosticsEventProcessor): void; emit(event: DiagnosticsEvent): void; getDroppedCount(): number; dispose(): Promise<void>; }

interface.name: InstrumentedAgentContext
interface.signature: { providerName: string; model: string; agentType: AgentType; projectId: string; engineId: string; taskId: string; taskType: string; }

interface.name: InstrumentedAgentProvider
interface.signature: implements IAgentProvider { constructor(inner: IAgentProvider, diagnostics: DiagnosticsQueue, context: InstrumentedAgentContext); updateContext(updates: Partial<Pick<InstrumentedAgentContext, 'taskId' | 'taskType'>>): void; executeTask(config, onProgress?): Promise<AgentTaskResult>; isAvailable(): Promise<boolean>; dispose(): Promise<void>; }

interface.name: InstrumentedLLMContext
interface.signature: { providerName: string; model: string; agentType: AgentType; projectId: string; engineId: string; taskId: string; taskType: string; }

interface.name: InstrumentedLLMProvider
interface.signature: implements IAIProvider { constructor(inner: IAIProvider, diagnostics: DiagnosticsQueue, context: InstrumentedLLMContext); initialize(config): Promise<void>; sendMessage(request, options?): Promise<AsyncIterable<MessageChunk>>; sendMessageSync(request): Promise<MessageResponse>; getCapabilities(): ProviderCapabilities; getModels(): Promise<ModelInfo[]>; dispose(): Promise<void>; }

interface.name: mapProviderName
interface.signature: (name: string | undefined): Provider — validates against known providers set, defaults to 'claude-code'

interface.name: IAgentProvider
interface.signature: { executeTask(config: AgentTaskConfig, onProgress?: AgentProgressCallback): Promise<AgentTaskResult>; isAvailable(): Promise<boolean>; dispose(): Promise<void>; }

interface.name: IAIProvider
interface.signature: { initialize(config): Promise<void>; sendMessage(request, options?): Promise<AsyncIterable<MessageChunk>>; sendMessageSync(request): Promise<MessageResponse>; getCapabilities(): ProviderCapabilities; getModels(): Promise<ModelInfo[]>; dispose(): Promise<void>; }

interface.name: ICostTracker
interface.signature: { recordUsage(usage: UsageRecordInput): Promise<UsageRecord>; generateReport(options: ReportOptions): Promise<CostReport>; ... }

standards.standard: Unit test coverage: 80% line, 75% branch, 85% function minimum
Critical paths (error handling, queue overflow, processor failure, drain concurrency, sanitization): 100% coverage
Mock external dependencies using vi.fn() and vi.mock()

test.description: Verify that emit() returns immediately without awaiting; executeTask returns before queue drains

test.idea: DiagnosticsQueue overflow drops oldest and increments droppedCount
test.description: Push maxQueueSize+1 events, verify first event is dropped, last event is present, and getDroppedCount() returns 1
Verify drainTimer.unref() is called after setInterval
Push events, call dispose(), verify processor receives remaining batch
Trigger drain while another drain is in flight; verify only one processor call occurs, second drain waits
Call setProcessor twice, verify logger.warn is called on the second call

test.idea: DiagnosticsQueue drain uses structured error logging
test.description: Processor throws, verify logger.warn called with error message instead of silent swallow
Verify ToolDiagnosticsEvent requires toolName, ProviderDiagnosticsEvent requires providerName, both share base fields
Verify agentType, projectId, engineId, taskId, taskType are required (not optional) on DiagnosticsEvent
Pass 600-char message, verify output is 500 chars + '...'
Pass message with 'Bearer abc123...', verify token is replaced with [REDACTED]
Pass message with 'sk-abcdef1234567890abcdef', verify key is replaced with [REDACTED]
Call executeTask, verify diagnostics.emit called with type provider:call including projectId, engineId, taskId, taskType before inner.executeTask resolves
Mock inner returning success, verify provider:complete event with latencyMs, success=true, costUsd, tokens
Mock inner throwing, verify provider:error event with typed DiagnosticsErrorCode, sanitized errorMessage, success=false
Call updateContext({taskId: 'new'}), then executeTask, verify new taskId in emitted events
Call isAvailable(), verify inner.isAvailable() is called
Verify class has initialize(), sendMessage(), sendMessageSync(), getCapabilities(), getModels(), dispose()
Mock streaming sendMessage, consume stream, verify provider:complete emitted on stream end with accumulated tokens

test.idea: InstrumentedLLMProvider sendMessage emits error on stream failure
test.description: Mock streaming sendMessage, throw during iteration, verify provider:error emitted with sanitized errorMessage
Verify typeof instance.isAvailable === 'undefined' at runtime; TypeScript compile check

test.idea: InstrumentedLLMProvider context includes projectId and engineId
test.description: Verify constructor context requires projectId and engineId, and they appear in emitted events
Verify sendMessage and sendMessageSync do not include request content in emitted events

test.idea: InstrumentedLLMProvider emits events with token counts
test.description: Mock sendMessageSync returning response with usage, verify tokens.input and tokens.output in complete event

test.idea: InstrumentedLLMProvider emits provider:error on sendMessageSync failure
test.description: Mock inner.sendMessageSync throwing, verify provider:error event with sanitized errorMessage
Verify mapProviderName('anthropic') returns 'anthropic', mapProviderName('unknown-xyz') returns 'claude-code'
Feed provider:complete event to processor, verify costTracker.recordUsage called with correct UsageRecordInput using mapProviderName
Mock costTracker.recordUsage throwing, verify logger.warn called and processing continues
Verify Provider type accepts 'opencode', 'z-ai', 'zen-mcp' without TypeScript errors
Verify cost-monitor AgentType is the same type as @tamma/shared AgentType

test.idea: Value validation: costUsd clamped to non-negative
test.description: validateCostUsd(-1) returns 0, validateCostUsd(5) returns 5

test.idea: Value validation: token count clamped to range
test.description: validateTokenCount(-1) returns 0, validateTokenCount(20_000_000) returns 10_000_000
validateErrorCode with 200-char string returns 100-char string
Verify DiagnosticsErrorCode accepts 'RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED' etc. but not arbitrary strings
